r: g̠ain, g̠ain-ě-moʻjama, g̠ain-ě-manqūt̤a, g, r, grasséyé, gh, g̠, abjad
def: (called g̠ain-ě-moʻjama, or g̠ain-ě-manqūt̤a), the twenty-fifth letter of the Urdū alphabet (the twenty-second of the Persian, and the nineteenth of the Arabic). It is a guttural g accompanied by a grating sound as in gargling, of which there is no example in English; — the Northumbrian r, and the French r in grasséyé are approximations to it. It occurs chiefly in Persian and Arabic words; but it is also found in some Hindūstānī words, in which it takes the place of an original ग g, or घ gh. It has no corresponding character in the Devanāgarī alphabet; and hence, when it occurs in words borrowed from the Persian or Arabic, it is usually represented by ग़ g̠. Its numerical value (according to abjad) is 1000.
more: called غين منقوطه or غين معجمه. The 19th letter of the Arabic, and 22nd of the Persian alphabet, has no corresponding character in Sanskrit. In abjad it stands for 100.

the nineteenth letter of the Arabic and twenty-second of the Persian alphabet, has no corresponding character in Sanskrit. It is one of the guttural letters; its sound being like that of the Northumberland r, or like a g uttered with compression deeply in the throat: and, it has the same relation to گ that خ has to ک. When used numerically, it stands for 1000. It is one of the radical letters. It is sometimes substituted for ز as گريغ for گريز and for گ as لغام for لگام. In conformity to Arabic orthography it is changed into ج as شلجم for شلغم and into ق as آقا for آغا.

Undetermined; not decided. gair-tahqīq , adj. 1. Vague; indefinite. 2. Undetermined; doubtful; not certain. gair-tarbiyat-yāftah, gair-tahzīb , adj. 1. Uncivilized; uneducate…

more: n. m. Grey bonduc nut; the fruit of the Guilandina Bondecella. gajge kī chārolī , n Grey bonduc seed. (?)
s. (dakh.) A sort of firework: see مينڐهل menḍhal (Qanoon-e-islam).

def: s. m. A string composed of several tassels made of the hair of a species of ox and suspended as an ornament from an elephantʼs neck, or it is fastened to a horseʼs ears, extending on both sides to the saddle.

ety: fr. Prk. गज्ज˚; S. गर्ज्, by redupl.
def: v.n. To sound, to rattle (as seed in a capsule, &c.); to buzz, to hum (as a crowd, or a swarm of bees, &c.); to swarm; to be populous or crowded (a town, &c.); to be full of business and bustle, to be lively, or brisk; — to be or become flabby.
more: v. n. 1. To rattle, as seed in its capsule. 2. To become flabby. 3. To be full of (worms).

def: adj. Thick, close, dense, imporvious (as a forest, &c.).
s.f. A female elephant.
more: n. f. An insect abounding in the rains ( kansalāī ).

def: s.f. corr. of gazī, q.v. s.m. One who engages in a warring expedition; one who fights against infidels; a warrior, a conqueror, a hero
subs: s.m. A man of war, a hero; (met.) a horse
s.m. A name of the nephew of Sultān Maḥmūd (he is worshipped as a saint); — g̠āzī-miyāṅ, or g̠āzī-miyān kī ćhaṛiyāṅ, A festival held in commemoration of Gāzī-miyāṅ (at the beginning of May).

def: s.m. Swampy ground.
s.m. A store, heap; plenty, abundance, opulence, wealth (stored up in one place)
more: n. m. P. ganj . 1. A heap; store. 2. Wealth. 1. Ab to kalejā tar ho gayā hogā, gajjhā dabā baiṭhe . 2. Kā kahūṅ gajjhā hāth lago, bhaujī, uṭkī uṭkī phirtī hau . I cant tell you, sister-in-law, it is as though some treasure she had nobbled somehow, she struts about so grand. gajjhā dabā baiṭhnā, gajjhā mārnā , v. n. To obtain fraudulently.
subs: or gajjhā mārnā, To obtain a vast amount of wealth or property by dishonest means, to make a great haul by frandulent means.

def: s. m. See گجموتي gaj-motī.
s. m. An elephant pearl (it being a popular idea of the Hindūs, that the finest pearls are to be found in the heads of elephants).
v.n. To sound; to roar; to rumble, thunder; — to be pleased, happy, cheerful, or delighted; — to revel and riot.
more: v. n. S. गर्जन, Pr. gajjan . 1. To roar (garajnā) . Bādī ṭāmāk īsā bājā, jūṅ ambar par Indar gājā . Rus. So loud the drummer played upon his drum, from Indra's courts the thunder seemed to come. 2. To revel and riot. 3. To lie heavy upon. Chhātī pe rin gājtā hai .
v. n. 1. To sound, to thunder. 2. To roar as a lion or other wild beast. 3. To be pleased, happy, cheerful, delighted.

def: s.f. Bole, earth.
s. m. An elephant-keeper.
s.m. Lamp-black (with which the eyes are painted; — syn. kājal).
adj. The master or rider of an elephant; the title of a rājā.
s.m. A carrot; leaf of a carrot; — a wrist-ornament (orig. 'carrot-shaped'), a bracelet (of gold, &c.); a necklace (of gold); — a kind of silk cloth (i.q. mashrūʻ); the waving lines on mashrūʻ
more: n. m. S. गाजर 1. The leaf of the carrot; carrots. 2. A gold necklace or bracelet originally carrot-shaped. 3. Silk cloth called mashrū. gajar-ḍaul ; A makhrūt , n. m. A cone.
s. m. 1. (see گاجر) The leaf of the carrot. 2. An ornament for the wrist, a bracelet, bracelet of flowers. 3. The waving lines on mashrūﻌ (a kind of silk-cloth).
subs: s.m. A kind of wreath or necklace (of flowers or of gold), &c., see gajrā.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀕𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀭 (gajjara), from Sanskrit गार्जर (gārjara, “carrot”). Doublet of گاجَر (gājar). Compare Marathi गजरा (gajrā).

def: s. m. 1. A wreath or necklace of flowers. 2. A gold ornament of the same form.
s. m. A large elephant.
s.f. = gāṅkar, q.v. s.m. A mass of dough baked on embers; an inferior kind of bread made of arhar and other hard grains.

ety: rt. of g̠ālīdan = g̠ārīdan, rt. Zend gar = S. गर् (ग्ट)
def: part. adj. Rolling, wallowing (used as last member of compounds).
more: 1. part. in compos. (of غاليدن) Rolling or wallowing on the side. 2. The nest of the bee or wasp. 3. A burrow or hole made by wild animals to breed in: a cave made by shepherds to keep their flocks in at night in the desert.

ety: S. गल्लः, prob. a Prk. word = S. गण्डः, through a form गड्डो?
def: s.m. The cheek; jowl; — a mouthful; a morsel; a handful (as of grain put into a mill at one time); — talk, conversation, speech (cf. Panjābī gal); a story; — prating, nonsense; — boasting, swagger; — a kind of tobacco (for chewing); a quid
more: n. m. S. गल्ल 1. The cheek. 2. A mouthful ( phaṅkā ). Sāre chane ek gāl kar gayā . 3. (Panj. gal ) Talk; conversation; speech. Gāl-vālā jite, māl-vālā hāre . Prov. The talkers win, the wealthy lose. 4. Jabber; nonsense. 5. Brag; boast; cheek. 6. A morsel; mouthful. 7. A handful of grain put in the mill at one time. gāl bajānā , v. n. 1. To produce a babbling sound by striking the mouth. 1. Jaise Har gun gāë, taise gāl bajāë . Prov. Or sing Har's praise, or cry ' Bam, bam .' 2. Bel-patr, aur āk, dhatūrā mandar meṅ le jāoge. Jab woh hoë haiṅ prasann jab tum donoṅ gāl bajāoge . Wood-apple leaves, dhatūrā, āk on altar lay, When he is pleased your swelling cheeks with joy shall play. 2. To talk nonsense. 3. To brag. gāl pichaknā , v. n. To sink (one's cheeks). gāl par gāl chaṛhnā , v. n. To become very fat. gāl phulānā ; Sant. pokpoko , v. n. To swell one's cheeks. gāl toṛnā, yā kāṭnā , Slang…
s. m. A sort of tobacco.
subs: To puff out the cheeks and produce a sound by striking them; — to prate, chatter, talk nonsense; to vaunt, vapour
(or gal-phaṭākī), s.f. Scolding, boasting, bragging, vapouring; talking nonsense; uttering falsehoods; — gal-phaṭākī karnā, To scold, &c.
To puff out the cheeks; to pout, sulk; — to puff and swell, to vaunt and vapour
To warm or foment the cheek; — s.m. A ceremony observed on the first arrival of the bridegroom at his father-in-law's house, where the relations warm his cheeks as a sign of welcome.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀕𑀮𑁆𑀮 (galla), from Sanskrit गल्ल (galla), possibly from Proto-Dravidian [Term?].

ety: prob. fr. S. गल्; cf. S. गल्दः
def: s.m. (Panjābī) Speech; word; saying; — thing, matter, affair (syn. bāt).
more: n. m. S. गल Pālī, galo . 1. Throat; neck. 2. ( gāl ) Cheek. 3. A mouthful. 4. ( Panj .) A word; saying; thing. 5. A fishing hook. (?) 6. Gallows; gibbet. gal-boṭī , n. f. The stomach and intestines. gal-baiyāṅ , n. m. Throwing the arm round one's neck. gal-baiyāṅ ḍālnā , v. a. To throw one's arms round another's neck. Gat baiyāṅ na ḍālo, re o bālmā! Wom. song. gal-pharā , n. m. The gills (of a fish, etc.). gal-phūṭ , n. Vaunting; brag. gal-phūlā , adj. Chubby-cheeked. gal-pheṛ , n. Swelling of the glands of the neck. gal-taṅg h. , v. n. Not to know if one stands on his head or his heels. gal-tanī , n. f. 1. The rope connected with the yoke on the bullock's necks. 2. Part of the headstall, or of a bridle. gal denā, yā lagānā , v. a. To hand ( phāṅsī denā ). gal-ḍab k. , Slang, v. a. To embezzle; bag. Sab āp gal-ḍab kar-ke chal dīyā. gal-sarī , n. f. A necklace worn by married women. gal-k…
s. m. Throat, neck. گل بہيان ڐالنا gal-bahiyāṅ ḍālnā, To throw oneʼs arms around anotherʼs neck.
s. (dakh.) Gallows, gibbet ( Harris ). گل دينا gal denā, To hang (a man: Harris ).
ex: Flowers have bloomed in the gardens.
wety: From Sanskrit गल (gala).

def: ( गल Throat, and پهٿا from پهٿنا) 1. To scold. 2. To lie.
s.f. Abuse, &c.
subs: (-kī), To use abusive language (to), to abuse.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀕𑀮𑁆𑀮 (galla), from Sanskrit गल्ल (galla), possibly from Proto-Dravidian [Term?].

def: s.m. A cave (esp. one made by shepherds to keep their flocks in at night); a hole, a burrow (of an animal); a bee-hive; a wasps' nest.
s.m. = گالا gālā, q.v. s.m. A pod of cotton; a flock of cotton; a ball of carded cotton.
s.m. The neck; the front part of the neck; throat; gullet; — voice; — a tube; funnel; syphon
more: n. m. 1. The throat; neck. 2. Voice. Jorū kā woh galā hai ki phūṭā ho jaise ḍhol . Nazīr. His wife's voice is like a cracked drum. 3. A tube; funnel. galā uṭhānā, yā k. lit . to raise the uvulā . To apply acrid substances, as pepper, etc. to the uvula of a child for a cold or cough. galā ānā , v. n. To be sore (the tonsils, throat). Galā ā jaëgā bachche kā mere, Chusātī roz khālī chhātiyāṅ ho! Wom. Nāznīṅ. A sore throat sure my child will get, To suck your dry breasts he is let. galā baṅdhānā , v. n. 1. To be bound in. 2. To incur responsibility, or a debt or liability. 3. To be yoked together, bound in wedlock. galā baṅdhnā , v. n. To be head and ears in debt. galā band h. , v. n. To be choked, suffocated. galā baiṭhnā yā paṛnā , v. n. To become hoarse. galā pakaṛnā , v. a. 1. To seize one by the neck or collar. 2. To feel a burning in the throat. Said of any acrid food or drink that co…
adj. 1. Melted. 2. Cooked; boiled; soft. Gosht kyoṅ na khāyā, ḍom kyoṅ na gāyā ? Ans. Galā * na thā. Do-sukhnā . A pun. 3. ( galā saṛā ) Rotted; rotten. galī miṭṭī . Rich mould.

1. s. m. the throat, neck. 2. Voice. 3. (from گلنا) adj. Rotten, melted, wasted. گلا اٿهانا galā uṭhānā, (lit. to lift up the throat) To apply acrid substances, such as pepper, &c. to the uvula of a child, when it becomes hoarse, or loses its voice from the relaxation of that part. گلا بندهانا galā bandhānā, To ensnare oneʼs self, especially in the bonds of love. گلا بيٿهنا يا پڙنا galā-baiṭhnā or -paṛnā, To be hoarse. گلا پهانسنا galā phāṅsnā, To hang, to choke. گلا دبانا galā dabānā, To strangle. گلا کاٿنا galā kāṭnā, To cut the throat. گلا گهوٿنا يا گهونٿنا galā ghoṭnā, or ghonṭnā, To throttle, to choke, to strangle, to hold by the throat. گلي پڙنا gale paṛnā, To seek the friendship of one who is averse from it. گلي پڙي بجاي سدهہ gale paṛī ba-jā-e sidh, We should make a virtue of necessity. گلي کا هارهونا gale kā hār honā, 1. To seek and persist in a quarrel or dispute, to tease, to p…
subs: (-kā), To raise the uvula (of a child, as a nurse does with her fingers in certain diseases); to apply acrid substances (as pepper, &c.) to the uvula (of a child, when it becomes hoarse, or loses its voice by the relaxation of the throat)
(-ko), To have sore throat (or inflamed tonsils)

(apnā), To put (one's) head in a noose, to ensnare oneself (in the bonds of love, &c.); to be or become bound (in); to incur responsibility or liability (for); to be tied (to), be yoked (to), be bound in wedlock
(-kā), The air-tube to be closed; to be choked or suffocated
adj. Having the voice or throat set; hoarse
or galā paṛṇā (-kā), The throat or voice to be or become set, to be or become hoarse
(-kā), To seize the throat (of), seize by the throat or collar; to throttle, to choke; — to seise or affect the throat (as anything astringent, &c.)
or galā phāṛ-ke bolnā, 'To rend the throat,' to vociferate, bellow, bawl, scream
v.n. To hang by the neck; to be strangled, to choke
v.n. The throat, or the glands of the neck, to swell
or galā dabānā (-kā), To squeeze or compress the throat (of), to throttle, strangle, choke
(-kā), To cut the throat (of); (fig.) to do violence (to), to bear hard (upon), to oppress, to wrong, to defraud
or galā masosnā (-kā) = galā ṭīpnā, q.v.
s.m. One who has a sweet voice, a sweet singer

or gale ćapeknā, or gale ḍālnā, v.t. To fasten to (or to throw on) the neck (of, -ke), to put upon the shoulders (of), lay to the charge (of); to fix an accusation or a slur wrongfully (upon); — to force (a thing upon one), to oblige one to accept
(-ke), 'To pass a knife across the throat' (of), to kill, slaughter (fig.); to torment, torture, harass

(-ke), To fall on the neck (of); to embrace; to seek; to seek the friendship (of one who is averse from it); to hang upon (in entreaty or enforcement), to importune, urge, dun; — to devolve (upon, as a care or business, &c.), to be obligatory or incumbent (on); to attach itself (to); to lie upon (one's) hands, to be a load, clog, or encumbrance
We should make a virtue of necessity
v.n. To go down (by way of) the throat, to be swallowed
v.n. = gale lagnā, q.v. — gale-kā ḍholnā, An amulet; — a load or burden, a clog, an encumbrance

A garland for the neck; a necklace; — (fig.) a child who constantly clings to his mother; a clog, an encumbrance; an importunate person; — a short woman married to a tall man; — gale-kā hār honā (-ke), To pursue one persistently; to seek and persist in a quarrel or dispute; to persecute, plague, tease; to importune; — to seek with ardour the friendship or affection (of); to persecute with kindness or attention
(-ko), To embrace (by putting another's arms round one's own neck)
(-ke), To hang on the neck (of a person) and weep
(-ke), To hang upon the neck (of), to embrace; — to hang upon, to urge, to importune
(-ke), To put upon the neck (of), &c. ( = gale bāṅdhnā); to importune
(-ke), A chain to be put on the neck (of); to be chained, be bound.
wety: From Sanskrit गल (gala). Ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *gʷel- (“throat”). Cognate with Latin gula (“throat”), English gullet, and Persian گلو (galu, “throat”).

ety: perf. part. of galnā; — Prk. गलिअओ; S. गलित = कः, rt गल्
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Melted, dissolved; softened, become soft (by boiling, &c.), cooked; soft; wasted, wasted away; rotted, rotten, &c. (see galnā)
wety: From Sanskrit गल (gala). Ultimately from Proto-Indo-European *gʷel- (“throat”). Cognate with Latin gula (“throat”), English gullet, and Persian گلو (galu, “throat”).

def: s.m. = گلا galā, q.v. s.m. A pod of cotton; a flock of cotton; a ball of carded cotton.
more: n. m. A complaint (اُلاﮩنا). gilah k., yā gilah guzārī k. , v. a. To complain.
n. m. A flock; herd; drove. galle-bān , n. m. A shepherd; herdsman. galle-bānī , n. f. Keeping flocks and herds.
s. m. See گلا galā, Throat, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گِلَه (gila).

def: s.m. Throat, neck, gullet; — noose, snare; — gallows, gibbet; (dialec.) a fish-hook; a drag-hook; a hook
subs: or gal-bāhī, or gal-bahī, s.f. Caressing by throwing the arms around the neck (of a person), embracing, embrace; — gal-bahiyāṅ (or baʼiyāṅ) ḍālnā, To throw the arms round the neck (of a person), to embrace
s.f. The wind-pipe, gullet; the stomach and intestines
adj. Having the throat (or voice) set; hoarse
s.f. Boasting, vaunting; boast; lying; arrogance; — scolding; — gal-phaṭākī karnā, To boast, vaunt; to lie; — to scold
s.f. Swelling of the glands of the neck
s.f. The rope connected with the yoke on the neck of a bullock; — part of the headstall of a bridle, &c.
(S. gala+yantrah), s.m. A sling (for the arm), or a handkerchief, &c. used as a sling
(-ko), To strangle, throttle, choke, to kill or execute by hanging, to hang; (dialec.) to hang on a hook
(-sarī, prob. S. śrī, or sadr̤iś, Prk. sari), s.f. A neck ornament (worn by women), a necklace of glass and gold beads with a gold coin in the middle
s.m. A cut-throat ( = gal-kaṭā); one who slaughters cattle
s.m. A rope with a loop or slip-knot at one end for fastening round the neck (of an animal); halter (for a horse)
To strain the throat in vain; to vociferate or bawl to no purpose
s.m. Seizing by the throat, throttling, choking; compression of the throat; — violence, injury; injustice
(-gandā = ganḍā = S. gaṇḍakah), s.m. 1˚ = gal-jandṛā, q.v.; — 2˚ = gala-gaṇḍ, q.v.
s.m. Enlargement of the glands of the neck; goitre; — the king's evil, scrofula
s.m. The adjutant (bird), Ardea argala (the name alludes to a pendulous fleshy purse hanging from the throat of the bird)
s.f. A garland for the neck, necklace; collar
(plur. gal-muććheṅ), s.f. Long curled whisker; hooked moustache
s.m. The hand at the throat; seizing by the throat, collaring, throttling.
ex: Flowers have bloomed in the gardens.
wety: From Sanskrit गल (gala).

def: s.m. A pod of cotton; a flock of cotton; a ball of carded cotton.
more: n. m. A flock of cotton. Gāle haiṅ battiyoṅ ko! Street cry. gālā sā, gālā, rūī kā gālā , adj. Soft and white as cotton; cottony. gālā se bāl . Hair like cotton. gāle k., yā banānā , v. a. To make flocks of carded cotton.
s. m. 1. A pod of cotton. 2. A ball of carded cotton.

def: s.m. A flock, herd, drove, bevy; — a till (see gallā)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گِلَه (gila).

ety: for A. غلبة, inf. n. of غلب 'to overcome,' &c.
def: s.m. Overcoming, overpowering, conquering, subduing, &c.; victory, conquest, ascendency, mastery, prevalence, predominance, superiority, advantage, superior power or force or influence; excess; assault; strength; success in a contest
more: n. m. See jīt , 1, 3, and sabqat . 1. Ascendancy; influence; mastery; predominance. 2. Excess. Galbah-i-khūn. galbah pānā , v. n. To get the upper hand; to be victorious. galba-i-rāë, galba-i-ārā . The opinion of the majority; a majority. galba k. , v. a. To overcome; predominate; override; gain the ascendancy; get the upperhand ( gālib ānā , 1, 4).
s. m. Superiority, prevalence, conquest, overcoming, advantage, assault, strength. غلبه کرنا g̠ẖalaba karnā, To overpower, overcome, conquer.
subs: (-par), To obtain the mastery (over), to be victorious, to get the better (of), &c.
(-par), To prevail (against), to overcome, overpower, conquer, subdue, to gain the ascendency, get the upper hand, &c.

def: s.f. (dialec.) = galāsī, q.v. s.f. A rope or fastening for the neck of an animal; halter (of a horse).

ety: S. गल, or more prob. अग्र+वह+इका
def: s.f. Fore-part, prow (of a boat), forecastle (of a ship).
more: s. f. The forecastle (of a boat, &c.), prow.

def: s.f. = galāwaṭ, and galāʼo, qq.v. s.f. The act of melting or dissolving; solution, &c. ( = galāʼo, q.v.).
s.f. A tumour in the neck; a swelling; a boil.
s. (dakh.) Liquefaction, melting.

ety: caus. of galnā; — galā˚ = Prk. गलावे˚ = S. गाल+आपय˚, caus. of rt. गल्
def: v.t. To melt, dissolve, fuse, liquefy; to soften (by boiling, or cooking, &c.); to waste, to squander away; to reduce or subdue (the body by mortification or austerities); to emaciate (a person, sickness or grief, &c.); — to cause to rot, &c. (see galnā)
more: v. n. S. गालन् See galnā , v. n.
( गालन or caus. of گلنا) v. a. 1. To melt, to dissolve. 2. To soften. 3. To waste away, to squander. گلا دينا galā denā, v. a. To waste.
subs: v.t. (intens.) To melt down, melt away; to waste, &c.

ety: prob. gal = gālī, 'abuse,'+andā = Prk. अंदओ = S. अत् (अन्त+कः)
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Abusive, scurrilous, foul-mouthed; — an abusive or foul-mouthed person.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Stentorophonic; — a person with an exceedingly loud or powerful voice.
s.m. Solution; fusion, liquefaction.
s.m. The red-ringed parrot or paroquet — a species of mainā (bird).
more: s. m. The name of a bird.

def: s.m. = gal-jandṛā, q.v. s.m. A pod of cotton; a flock of cotton; a ball of carded cotton.
s.f. A rope or fastening for the neck of an animal; halter (of a horse).

ety: inf. n. of غلط 'to make a mistake,' &c.
def: adj. Wrong, erroneous, incorrect, inaccurate; untrue, false
more: adj. 1. Wrong; erroneous; inaccurate; incorrect. 2. Untrue; false. galat-ul-ām . A popular error or fallacy. Galat-ul-ām fasīh . Prov. Vulgar errors pure idioms. galat-ul-avām . A vulgar error or fallacy. galat-bardār , Ped. for H. khurchnī , An eraser. galat ṭhairānā , v. a. 1. To prove to be wrong or erroneous. 2. To show the fallacy of; invalidate; defeat; overthrow; demolish; expose ( tardīd k .). galat khabar denā , v. a. To misinform; give false intelligence. galat rāë . A wrong or erroneous opinion. galat samajhnā , v. a. To misapprehend; misconceive; misunderstand; misjudge. galat-fahmī , n. f. A misconception; misunderstanding; misapprehension. galat k. , v. a. To annual; set at naught.

1. adj. Wrong, erroneous. 2. s. Errour, mistake. غلط العام g̠ẖalat̤u-l-āﻌmm, A popular mistake, a vulgar errour. غلط فـﮩمي g̠ẖalat̤-fahm-ī, s. f. Mistake, misconception. غلط کر g̠ẖalat̤-kār, adj. Delusive. غلط کاري g̠ẖalat̤-kār-ī, s. f. Delusion, deception. غلط گو g̠ẖalat̤-go, A false relater. غلط گوٴي g̠ẖalat̤-go-ī, s. f. Relating falsehoods. غلط نويس g̠ẖalat̤-navīs, An erroneous writer.
subs: or g̠alat̤uʼl-ʻawām, A popular mistake, a vulgar error or fallacy
v.t. To prove to be wrong or erroneous; to show the fallacy of, expose the error of
v.n. To make a mistake, to mistake, to err; — v.t. To make wrong, to regard as erroneous; to set at naught; to annul
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غَلَط (ġalat), borrowed from Arabic غَلَط (ḡalaṭ).

ety: g̠alat̤, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. A mistake, an error; inaccuracy; miscalculation; fallacy; misstatement; misapprehension, misconception; an oversight, a slip
more: n. f. See bhūl . 1. Inaccuracy; miscalculation; erratum ; 2. A misstatement. 3. An oversight; a slip; miss. 4. A fallacy. 5. See galat-fahmī. galatī paṛnā , v. n. more: n. f. S. गालि Abusive language; abuse. 1. Sau gāliyoṅ kā ek gālā banāyā aur uṛā dīyā . Prov. Abuses one hundred he gave me, as a cotton ball I flung them all from me. 2. Phīkī hī nīkī lage kahiye samaë bichār, Sab ko man harikhat kare jīūṅ bivāh meṅ gār . A vapid speech that with the hour doth chime May please, as coarse songs please at wedding time. 3. Gālī ( gāṛī ) degā to bail dhī degā, aur hāṅknevālā sāth degā . Boys. If you give me abuse (a cart) you 'll bullocks give, and a driver too to boot. 4. Gālī aur tarkārī khāne hī ke vāste haiṅ . Abuse and vegetables are swallowed both. gālī denā , v. a. To abuse; call names. Jo mai ko gālī daī hogī in logan meṅ, Rām duhāī! Ab yeh rār nai hogī . Song. Ṭhumrī . If you give me abuse before these people all, by Rām I swear, a quarrel new there 'll be. gālī khānā ; Sant. ruhet ocho , v. n. To get abuse. Gālī o mār khāte haiṅ kauṛī ke vāste, Sha…
s. f. Abuse. گالي گلوج gālī galauj, s. f. Reciprocal abuse, brawl. گاليان gāliyāṅ, Indecent songs (sung) at marriages. گالي ديني gālī denī, To abuse, revile, call names, scold.

n. f. The short stick which is struck in tip cat by the longer one called ḍanḍā. gillī-ḍanḍā , W.; gullī-ḍaṅḍā yā ṭāṅṛ , E.; tāl gullī , Tir. n. m. The game of tip-cat. gillī-ḍanḍā khelnā , v. a. 1. To play at tip-cat. 2. To idle away one's time. gilliyāṅ ghaṛnā , v. n. To argue or debate uselessly.

def: part. adj. Overcoming, overpowering, victorious, triumphant, prevailing, predominant, prevalent; superior, surpassing, excelling; — most probable, most likely; — s.m. The most, the most or greater part, the generality
more: adj. غلب overcome. See zabar-dast (1, 3). 1. Overcoming; overpowering; predominant; paramount. 2. Successful; triumphant; victorious. 3. Excelling; surpassing. 4. Probable; likely. gālib ānā, rahnā, yā h. , v. n. See jītnā and zer k. , 1. To surmount; master; vanquish. 2. To gain, attain, secure, or win a point or object. 3. To frustrate; foil; discomfit ( toṛnā , 13, 17). 4. To excel; surpass. Phr. To bear away the bell; to beat hollow; to get the whip hand of; to turn up trumps; to have the game in one's hand; to have on the hip. gālib ānā phand yā fareb se . To circumvent; overreach; cheat; trick. gālib thā . It was likely or expected. gālib hai , adv. It is probable.
part. act. (of غلب) Overcoming, overpowering, victorious, predominant, excelling, superiour, most likely. غالب هونا g̠ẖālib-honā, To overcome, to excel, to conquer, to be victorious (over).
subs: or g̠ālib rahnā, or g̠ālib honā (-par), To prevail (against), to overcome, vanquish, sudue, defeat, beat, foil; to surmount, master; to prove or be superior (to), to surpass, excel; to win, gain
v.t. To make or render (one) victorious, &c. (over, -par), to give (one) the mastery (over), to allow (one) to prevail (over or against)

ety: Prk. गलणअं; S. गलनीयं, rt. गल्
def: v.n. To drop, fall (as fruit, flowers, or plaster from a wall, &c.); to leak, to run or ooze; to sweat; to sink (as a pier or a well, &c.); to melt, dissolve; to be or become dissolved; to be boiled or cooked till soft (meat, vegetables, &c.); to waste away, to become emaciated or lean (from sickness or grief, &c.); to be wasted, be thrown away, be dissipated or squandered; to be forfeited, be lost (as a stake, &c.); to decay; to putrefy, rot, become rotten; to fall away, to perish; to vanish, cease, become extinct; — to be frozen or frost-bitten; — to be sold in great quantities
more: v. n. S. गलन. 1. (Sant. le ) To melt (پگلنا 1). 2. To be boiled, cooked. Chāval gal gaë . 3. To rot; spoil. Phal gal gayā . 4. To mortify; drop off. Uṅgliyāṅ gal gaīṅ . 5. To sink (a well or pier); settle down. Golā galnā . 6. To be wasted, thrown away. 7. To be forfeited, lost. Dāoṅ gal gaë . 8. To be sold in great quantities. 9. To be frozen or frost-bitten.
v. n. To melt, dissolve, waste, be dissolved, be wasted away.

ety: for A. g̠amm, inf. n. of غمّ 'to cover, veil,' &c.
def: adj. Sorrowful, grieved, afflicted, sad
more: n. m. غم concealed, grieved. 1. Dolor; heartache; woe ( ranj , 1). 1. Gam ke māre roṭī nahīṅ bhātī . Grief makes my food unpleasant to me. 2. Allah hai to kyā gam hai? Prov. Saying. Why grieve should I while God is nigh? 3. Ātā ho to use hāth se na dīje, jātā ho to uskā gam na kīje . Prov. What comes to you it ne'er resign, what's gone from you for it ne'er pine. 2. Mourning ( sog , 2). 3. Care; concern. 1. Agar sar khulā hai to kuchh gam nahīṅ, Jo kurtī hai mailī to mahram nahīṅ . Mīr Hasan. Her head is bare, she can abide it, Her jacket soiled, she will not heed it. 2. Gam na dārī buz bikhar . P. Prov. If care you'd have, go, buy a goat! 3. Na jīne kī shādī, na marne kā gam . Prov. Nor pleasure in life, nor care to die. gam-khuār, gam-khār , adj. lit . devouring sorrow. Condoling; sympathizing. gam-khuār , n. m. One who feels for another; a comforter. gam-khuārī , n. f. Sympathy; condo…

(properly g̠ẖamm ) s. m. Grief, sorrow, concern. غم کهـانا g̠ẖam-khānā, 1. To grieve, to mourn. 2. To have patience. غم آلوده g̠ẖam-ālūda, adj. Grieved. غم خوار g̠ẖam-ḵẖẉār, Devouring sorrow, i. e. 1. adj. Afflicted, sad. 2. Condoling, condoler, pitying. 3. An intimate friend, a comforter. غمخوارک g̠ẖam-ḵẖẉārak, s. A heron. غمخواري g̠ẖam-ḵẖẉār-ī, s. f. 1. Affliction. 2. Real friendship, commiseration, condolence, comforting. غمخورک g̠ẖam-ḵẖụrak, A heron. غمديده g̠ẖam-dīda, adj. Accustomed to grief, afflicted. غمرسيده g̠ẖam-rasīda, adj. Afflicted. غمزدگي g̠ẖam-zada-gī, s. f. Affliction. غمزده g̠ẖam-zada, adj. Grieved, afflicted. غمکده g̠ẖam-kada, s. m. House of sorrow. غم کرنا g̠ẖam-karnā, To lament, to grieve. غم کش g̠ẖam-kash, Enduring grief, continuing in grief. غمگسار g̠ẖam-gusār, A remover of grief, a consoler, a confidant, an intimate friend. غمگساري g̠ẖam-gusār-ī, s. f. Alleviation…
subs: adj. Sorrow-cutting, removing or dispelling grief, &c.

adj. & s.m. (One) upon whom sorrow has come, afflicted, &c. ( = g̠am-zada)
adj. & s.m. Sorrow-stricken, afflicted, grieved, sad; — one who is afflicted or suffers sorrow, a sufferer

s.m. Drowner of sorrow or care; diversion, amusement, pleasure, sport, pastime; wine; — g̠am-g̠alat̤ karnā (apnā), To forget (one's) grief or sorrow; to divert (one's mind), to comfort or solace (oneself)
(-par), To grieve (for), to mourn (over), to lament, bewail
adj. & s.m. Enduring grief, continuing in grief; — one who endures sorrow, &c.
(apnā), To suffer grief, to grieve, mourn; — to feel or show sympathy (for, -kā); — (colloq.) to swallow, or to restrain (one's) grief, to have patience
adj. & s.m. Removing grief, consoling; — a remover of grief, consoler; intimate friend, confidant
s.f. Removal or alleviation of grief; consolation; sympathy, friendship
adj. Sorrowful, grieved, sad, dejected, disconsolate
ex: اُس کی موت پر گہرا غم منایا گیا۔
uskī maut par gahrā ġam manāyā gayā.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian غَم (ġam), borrowed from Arabic غَمّ (ḡamm). First attested in c. 1503 as Middle Hindi غم (ġm /⁠ġam⁠/).

def: s.f. A deep tone; sound (of a musical instrument, e.g. bīn-kī gamak); roll (of a drum); a violent shake or clash (in music); — a beat, march; — scent, odour, fragrance, aroma
more: n. f. 1. The roll of the drum. 2. A beat; a march. (?) 3. (Tir.) Smell. gamak-chhand . A metre, each line consisting of five short syllables.
s. f. Sound of the kettledrum, violent shake or clash in musick.
subs: s.m. A kind of metre (each line consisting of five short syllables).

def: s.m. Going, act of going; manner of going, gait; going to, approaching; intercourse (with)
more: s. m. Going, the act of going.
subs: (˚na+ag˚), s.m. Coming and going; intercourse; passing and repassing; thoroughfare
To move, go, pass; to have sexual intercourse (with, -ke sāth).

def: v.t. To pass (time, &c.).
more: (caus. of گمنا) v. a. To pass (time).
wety: From گم (gum, “lost, missing”) + آ (ā, causative augment) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: part. adj. What may be gone to or reached, approachable, accessible; passable; attainable; practicable; requiring to be traversed; traversable; movable; perceptible; — relating to sexual intercourse.
s.m. corr. of gaṅwār, q.v. s.m. (f. -ī), A villager, countryman, peasant, rustic; a clown, boor, churl; a low-bred person; — a stupid fellow, a dolt; — adj. Boorish, clownish; stupid ( = gaṅwārī)
subs: s.m. A villager's club; — (fig.) a blockhead, dolt, lout, fool.

def: s.m. A road, path.
more: n. f. 1. A society; company; class. Are! ko mānas mhārī gammat kā sai uṛai ? Rus. Is there any one here I would like? 2. A confederacy (اتفاق 5, 6). 3. Use; profit. 4. A bargain.
s. m. (dakh.) Occupation, business: see شغل shug̠ẖl.
s. m. (dakh.) Diversion, recreation, amusement, fun, play, luxury, pleasure.

ety: Prk. गम्मत्ती = गम्य(ते)+आप्ति (i.e. āpi, caus. augment.+ti)
def: s.f. The passing of time pleasantly; any pleasant object or occupation; diversion, recreation, amusement, pleasure, fun, play; — occupation, business; — a society, company, class; a confederacy; — use; profit; — a bargain.
adj. (dialec.) Amusing, diverting, entertaining, funny; cheerful, sportive, gamesome.
more: n. f. Cor. of Eng. Dimity. Imṭī gimṭī morā man hī na bhāve, Nainū kī chādar maṅgāo, balmā! Coarse cloth, my husband, is not to my mind, A muslin mantle, darling, for me find!
adj. (dakh.) Gamesome, sportive, funny, cheerful ( Harris ).

def: v.t. (rustic) corr. of gaṅwānā, q.v. v.t. To lose; to lose or miss (a road, or one's way); to throw away, to get rid, of; to spend in vain; to waste, squander; to pass or spend (time); to trifle or fritter away (time, &c.)

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Deep (lit. and fig.); deep-sounding, hollow-toned; profound, sagacious; grave, sedate, serious, solemn; earnest; secret, mysterious; — thick, dense, impervious, impenetrable; inscrutable; — a serious person, &c.; — a large boil on the back (?).
more: adj. S. गभीर; ; Pālī, gabhīro . 1. Deep; profound. 2. Grave; serious; sedate; solemn. gambhīr-tā , n. f. 1. Depth; profundity. 2. Gravity; seriousness.
n. m. A large boil on the back.
wety: From Sanskrit.

def: adj. See گمبهير gambhīr.
more: adj. 1. Deep (as water, but also used metaphorically). 2. Serious, thoughtful, sedate, grave.
wety: From Sanskrit.

def: adj. 1. Deep (as water, but also used metaphorically). 2. Serious, thoughtful, sedate, grave.
s.f. = S گمبهيرتو गम्भीरत्व gambhīratva, s.m. Depth (of water, or sound, &c.), profundity; profoundness; sagacity; earnestness; seriousness, gravity; dignifiedness; — mysteriousness, &c.
s.m. = S گمبهيرتا गम्भीरता gambhīratā, s.f. Depth (of water, or sound, &c.), profundity; profoundness; sagacity; earnestness; seriousness, gravity; dignifiedness; — mysteriousness, &c.

s.m. = gambhīratā, and gahrāʼo, qq.v. s.f. = S گمبهيرتو गम्भीरत्व gambhīratva, s.m. Depth (of water, or sound, &c.), profundity; profoundness; sagacity; earnestness; seriousness, gravity; dignifiedness; — mysteriousness, &c.
s.m. (dialec.) A towel (syn. aṅgoćhā).
more: n. f. अङ्गप्रोञ्छत A towel ( aṅgochhā ).

ety: g̠am, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Sorrow, pain; death; mourning
more: n. f. 1. Death; demise ( intiqāl , 5). Shādī aur gamī sāth hai . Prov. Pain and pleasure with us ever. 2. Mourning. gamī h. , v. n. To happen (a death).
adj. Sorrowful, grieved. ¶ g̠ẖam-ī, s. f. (dakh.) Grief, sorrow.

def: s.m. (f. -inī), A passenger; traveller.

ety: gam, q.v.+in or ī = Zend aff. aêna = S. इन् (ई)
def: adj. Sorrowful, grieved, sad, dejected, unhappy.
adj. (f. gāminī), Going, moving on, or in, or towards, moving in any peculiar manner; going in (to), having intercourse with (used as last member of compounds).
s.m. = gamī, q.v. s.f. Sorrow, pain; death; mourning

ety: gam, q.v.+in or ī = Zend aff. aêna = S. इन् (ई)
def: adj. Sorrowful, grieved, sad, dejected, unhappy.
more: adj. Sorrowful, grieved. ¶ g̠ẖam-ī, s. f. (dakh.) Grief, sorrow.

def: adj. (f. gāminī), Going, moving on, or in, or towards, moving in any peculiar manner; going in (to), having intercourse with (used as last member of compounds).
adj. f. Going, moving.
E. n. f. The duties of barbers and pirohits , or the fees paid to them for taking messages or presents to distant friends.

ety: act. part. of غمض 'to be unperceived, or hidden,' &c.
def: adj. Obscure, recondite, abstruse, enigmatical, difficult to be understood.
more: (part. act. of غمض) adj. Abstruse, difficult to be understood, enigmatical.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Fragrant, odoriferous, aromatic, spicy.
s.m. A large and deep earthen vessel with a broad mouth; a pan (syn. nāṅd).
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit कुम्भ (kumbha).

ety: intens. n. fr. غمز 'to make a sign'; 'to calumniate,' &c.
def: s.m. An informer, a calumniator, backbiter; a tale-bearer.
more: Poet. n. m. Superl of غمز A backbiter ( chugal , 1); a blab. gammāzī , n. f. Backbiting ( bad-goī ).
(adj. superl. of غمز) s. m. An informer, a talebearer, backbiter.

ety: g̠ammāz, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Informing (against), talebearing, backbiting, traducing.

ety: gam˚ = Prk. गम्म(इ) = S. गम्यते, pass. of rt. गम्
def: v.n. To go by, to pass, elapse; to be passed or spent (as time); — to be lost; to go astray (cf. gum); — to procrastinate.
more: s. m. Going, the act of going.
v. n. (dakh.) To be listless or unable to act.

def: s.m. Going, proceeding.
adj. (f. -ā), Going, moving, locomotive (used as last member of compounds).

v.t. = gaṅwānā, q.v. v.t. To lose; to lose or miss (a road, or one's way); to throw away, to get rid, of; to spend in vain; to waste, squander; to pass or spend (time); to trifle or fritter away (time, &c.)

part. adj. Approachable, accessible, &c. ( = gamanīya, q.v.). part. adj. What may be gone to or reached, approachable, accessible; passable; attainable; practicable; requiring to be traversed; traversable; movable; perceptible; — relating to sexual intercourse.
s.f. = S گميتو गम्यत्व gamyatva, s.m. Accessibleness; traversableness; attainableness; perceptibility.
s.m. = S گميتا गम्यता gamyatā, s.f. Accessibleness; traversableness; attainableness; perceptibility.

ety: for A. غمزة g̠amzat, n. of un. fr. g̠amz, inf. n. of غمز 'to make a sign,' &c.
def: s.m. A sign with the eye, a wink; an amorous glance, ogling; coquetry, affectation
more: n. m. 1. A wink; an amorous glance; ogling. 2. Affectation; coquetry. Aisā gamza alag karte haiṅ . I like not such affectation.
s. m. A wink, an amorous glance, coquetry, ogling, cast of the eye. غمزه زن g̠ẖamza-zan, adj. Ogling.

def: s.m. Singing; a song
more: n. m. S. गान Singing. gān-biddyā , n. m. The art of singing.
subs: s.f. The science of vocal music.

def: s.m. A heavy stone for pressing oil, &c.; a stone or wooden mortar; — (in Urdū), adj. Dead drunk.
more: 1. s. m. A heavy stone for pressing oil, &c. 2. (in Hindī) adj. Dead drunk.

ety: inf. n. of غنی 'to be or become free from want,' &c.
def: s.f. Freedom from need or want; competence, sufficiency; richness, wealthiness; riches, wealth; — content.
more: s. f. Riches, wealth, content. ¶ g̠ẖinā, s. f. Song, singing.

ety: gā˚ = Prk. गा(इ) or गाअ(इ) = S. गाय(ति), rt. गै
def: s.m. Singing, &c.
more: v. a. S. गै Pālī, gayatī . 1. To sing; chant. 1. Ronā aur gānā kise nahīṅ ātā? Prov. Who knows not how to cry and sing? 2. Uttam gānā, aur maddham bajānā . Prov. Best is the songster's art, the musician's next. 3. Gāo, bajāo, kauṛī na pāo! Prov. Sing and play, not a sou you 'll get! 4. Srī Rādhe piyārī, de ḍālo na bansī morī! Kāhe se gāūṅ, piyārī, kāhe se bajāūṅ, kāhe se lāūṅ gaiyāṅ gherī? Brij. song. Rās lilā. Give me my pipe, Rādha, my love! without my pipe how can I sing, how play, how call my herds? 2. To celebrate; make known. 3. To sing the praises of; to applaud. 4. To say; relate; recite; describe. Apnī apnī sab gīte haiṅ . Prov. Each one has his own say. gānā bajānā , n. m. Singing and playing; music and song. gā'yan, gāyak ; Sant. gaënāhā , n. m. A singer; songster.
subs: (-ko), To have acquired the faculty of singing, to be able (or to know how) to sing
s.m. Singing and playing; song and music.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit गान (gāna).

ety: prob. fr. Zend gaona = S. गुण
def: adj. affix, Fold (it is affixed to numerals, and is often redundant, e.g. panj-gāna, 'five-fold'; 'five').
more: An affix to Persian numerals, as panj-gānah . Five, or five fold.

def: s.m. Flock, troop, company, host, multitude, number, series; genus; group, class, category; tribe, sect, party; — troops or classes of inferior deities (esp. certain troops of demigods considered as Śiva's attendants and under the special superintendence of the god Gaṇeśa); — a small body of troops, consisting of three gulmas (i.e. twenty-seven chariots, twenty-seven elephants, eighty-one horse, and one hundred and thirty-five foot); — a series or group of asterisms or lunar mansions classed under three heads (that of the gods, that of men, and that of the Rākshasas or demons); — (in Arith.) a number; — (in Prosody) a division of time ( = 30 kasṭhas or about eight seconds); a measure of four instants or short syllables; — (in Gram.) a series of roots or words belonging to the same rule and called after the first word of the series (e.g. ad-ādi, the Gaṇa ad, &c., or the whole series of roots of the second class); — (gaṇ is not unfrequently used as an affix to form plurals, e.g. tārā-gaṇ, The starry host, the stars)
more: n. m. S. गुण 1. Property (صفت). 2. Merit; virtue; excellence. 1. Simiṭi simiṭi jal bhare talāvā, Jimi sad guṅṛ sajjan paë āvā . Rāmāyan. As water from all sides a tank doth fill, So crowds about a holy man flock still. 2. Nij gun sunat shravaṅṛ sakuchāhiṅ, Par guṅr sunat adhik harkhāhiṅ . Rāmāyan. Saints shrink abashed when they their own praise hear, Another's praise fills them with joy sincere. 3. Chhorā! tere gun mere peṭ meṅ bhare . Brij. Boy! I know you every inch. 3. Skill; cleverness. 4. Kindness ( kirpā ). Kamar-ṭuṭṭī kī davā batā do hakīm jī! jab tak jītī rahī gun mānūṅgī . Wom. song. A remedy, physician skilled, for weak loins give! My gratitude I'll show through all the years I live. 5. Effect; advantage ( phal 4, 5). 6. Cause; reason. 7. ( Arith .) Multiplication ( zarab , 3). 8. A tracking rope. gunā bād , n. m. Thanks; gratefulness. gun augan , Merits and demerits. gun-bāch…
s. m. 1. A troop, tribe, flock, class, multitude. 2. Troops of inferiour deities considered as Sivaʼs attendants, and under the especial superintendance of Gaṇesa; Sivaʼs attendants.

s. m. 1. Quality, attribute, merit, skill, understanding, virtue, mode, method, manner. 2. A property of humanity (see under رجوگن rajo-guṇ ). 3. A string, rope, small track-rope. 4. Favour, kindness. گن چهانڐنا guṇ chhānḍnā, To pass over a personʼs good qualities. گن کا پلٿا دينا guṇ kā palṭā denā, To repay a benefit. گن کرنا guṇ karnā, To benefit. گنگاهک guṇ-gāhak (or dakh. گن گايکا guṇ-gāʼekā ) s. m. Discerner of merit, patron of learning. گن ماننا guṇ mānnā, To acknowledge a favour. گن ندهان guṇ-nidhān, Abode of skill or merit; skilful.
subs: vulg. gaṇ-pat, s.m. 'Lord of the inferior deities,' an epithet of Gaṇeś; — a proper name
s.m. Public property; common stock; — a variety of articles
s.m. pl. The classes of demi-gods or inferior deities who are the attendants of Śiva (see gaṇ)
s.m. The leader or chief of the attendants of any god; the chief of Śiva's attendants, the god Gaṇeśa

vulg. gaṇes, gaṇes (gaṇa + īśa), s.m. lit. 'Lord or chief of the troops' (of inferior deities; see gaṇ); name of the son of Śiva and Pārvatī (he is the god of wisdom, and the remover of obstacles; hence he is invoked at the commencement of all undertakings, literary composition, &c. Most Hindū literary compositions begin with the phrase śrī-gaṇeśāya-namah, 'reverence to the divine Gaṅeś); — a name of the god Śiva; — a proper name (of men)
s.f. A Hindū festival observed in honour of Ganeś on the fourth of Aghan
s.m. 'A cow-dung image of Ganeś'; a fat dullard; — a lubberly fellow; an arrant fool.
ex: اُس کے کوئی اَچّھے گُن ہَیں ہی نَہِیں۔
us ke koī acche gun ha͠i hī nahī̃.
He has no good qualities at all.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit गुण (guṇá).

def: s.m. Plunderers; enemies.
s.m. (f. -ī), One who reckons, a calculator; an arithmetician; — an astrologer; one who calculates (or casts) nativities, &c.
s.f. The wife of an astrologer (see gaṇak).
s.m. Offensive breath; bad smell from the mouth.
more: n. m. Cont. of P. gandhā- dhanī . A bad smell from the mouth.

def: (inf. n. of غنم 'to obtain, get, or take spoil'), s.m.(?) Plundering, carrying off; plunder, spoil, booty (syn. g̠anīmat).
s.m. (f. -ā), Reckoning, counting; enumeration; calculation; adding, addition
(see گنا gannā ) s. (dakh.) Counter, numberer.
part. adj. To be counted or reckoned, calculable; requiring counting; to be classed.
s.f. Arithmetic (see also gaṇatva).
s.m. (f. gaṇatā), s.m. The forming a class, or multitude, or assemblage; the belonging to a party or faction; the condition (or the office) of an attendant of Śiva (see gaṇ); — arithmetic.

ety: gannā, q.v.+orī = Prk. उल्लिआ = S. उल+इका
def: s.f. A bulrush; reed-grass.
s.f. = گانڙ gāṅṛ, q.v. s.f. The anus; the privities
more: s. f. The anus, privities. گانڐ غلط gānḍ-g̠ẖalat̤, adj. Dead, stupid.
subs: (-kī), To get into a funk, to be in a great fright
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit गण्ड (gaṇḍá). Cognate with Assamese গাঁৰ (gãr).

ety: Pehl. gand; Zend gaiñti; S. गन्ध
def: s.f. Smell, bad smell; stink, stench; filth, ordure.
more: s. f. Stink, filth, ordure, smell.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit गन्ध (gandha, “smell, stink”). Cognate with Persian گنده (gande, “stenchy”).

def: s.m. (Dakh.) The upper and forepart of the neck (under the jaw).
s.f. contrac. of gāṅḍ or gāṅṛ (used chiefly in comp.).
adj. = گنده ganda, q.v. s.m. Sugar-cane, &c. (i.q. gannā, q.v.). s.m. Sugar-cane, Saccharum officinarum; — (dialec.) a reed; a cane; reed-grass (rustic forms are gaṇḍā; gāṅḍā)
more: n. m. H. gāoṅ . Land situated nearest to the village.

n. m. S. गण्ड. 1. Four kauris or four pice. 2. The ring round the neck of a dove. 3. A string made of three colored threads knotted together worn about the neck by women and children as an amulet to protect them from the evil eye. Parāë ganḍoṅ ke bharose na rahnā ! Prov. Put not your trust in charms! (Use your own strength). 4. Large conch shells or heart-shaped plates of gold or silver fixed on a string and hung round the necks of horses and cattle as ornaments and amulets. 5. (Brij) Dependance. Unke āë par kyā ganḍā hai? What dependance can you place on his coming? ganḍe-dār , adj. 1. Spotted; striped. 2. Irregular. ganḍe tāvīz k. , v. a. To exorcise by means of amulets.

def: s.m. (dialec. or rustic) Land adjacent (or lying nearest) to a village (gāʼoṅ; S. grāma).

s.m. An aggregate of four 'cowries,' or four pice (paisā); the number four, counting by fours; — disjunction, separation; a joint; a reed; sugar-cane (syn. gannā); — a ring, circle (esp. a magic circle, or one which is supposed to act as a charm or amulet, i.e. as an 'obstacle or impediment' to the influence of evil spirits, &c.); — a charmed cord (generally of three coloured threads knotted together) bound round the neck (or wrist, or ankle) to avert or remove demoniac influence, or the fascination of an evil eye; — the string which teachers of particular arts or crafts (as singers, dancers, conjurers, &c.) bind round a finger or the wrist of the right arm of their pupils; — a cord of black thread (or large conch shells, or plates of gold and silver of an acuminate heart shape, strung together and) fastened round the neck of a horse as an ornament or an amulet; — the ring or collar round the neck of a turtle-dove or ring-dove; — (local) dependence (e.g. unke āʼe-par kyā gaṇḍā hai); — a mark, spot, stripe
more: s. m. 1. (for गंड ) A ring, a circle, a kind of horse-collar. 2. A knotted string tied round the neck of a child, &c. as a preservative against evil. 3. (for गंडक ) Denotes the number four.
subs: v.t. To charm, exorcise (by means of amulets); to bewitch

def: s.m. = گنڐا gaṇḍā, q.v. s.m. Sugar-cane, &c. (i.q. gannā, q.v.). s.m. Sugar-cane, Saccharum officinarum; — (dialec.) a reed; a cane; reed-grass (rustic forms are gaṇḍā; gāṅḍā)
adj. (f. -ī), Stinking, fetid; rotten, addled (as an egg), putrid; rancid; — filthy, dirty; — foul, obscene, indecent (speech); — harsh, sour, vile (temper)
more: adj. S. गन्ध. 1. Stinking; fetid. 2. Filthy; dirty. Gandī boṭī kā gandā shurvā . Prov. Bad meat, bad 3. Obscene; indecent. Gandī bāt . 4. Ill-tempered; sour. Gandah mizāj. ganda-birozā , n. 1. The resin of the Boswellia thurifera . 2. A kind of pine (P. longifolia ). 3. Frankincense. gandā pānī , n. m. 1. Dirty water. 2. Semen; sperma genitale . 3. Urine. 4. Wine. Kyā kahūṅ maiṅ ki kaisā kām kīyā! Gande pānī se ā-ke dhār dīyā . Wom. Jān Sāhab. ganda-dahan , adj. Having a stinking breath. gandā-dahanī , n. f. Stinking breath. ganda kām , n. m. 1. Dirty work. 2. Sexual intercourse. ganda k. , v. a. 1. To foul; dirty. 2. To pollute; defile. 3. To stain; disgrace. gandī gandī bāteṅ k. , v. a. To use obscene zabān yā muṅh gandah k . To use foul language.

adj. Fetid, stinking. گنده بہار ganda-bahār, s. f. Rain which falls in the cold weather. گنده دهن ganda-dahan, adj. Having stinking breath. گنده فيروزه ganda-fīroza, s. m. The name of a drug. (Olibanum).
subs: adj. & s.m. Having stinking armpits; — one whose armpits emit a stench
s.f. Rain which falls in the cold weather
s.m. Stinking or dirty water; — semen; — urine; — wine
s.f. The having stinking breath; stinking breath
or ganda-biroza, s.m. Olibanum, male frankincense, the resin of the Boswellia thurifera; the crude turpentine or resin of the cīr or pine, P. longifolia
v.t. To make foul or dirty, to foul, to soil; to defile, pollute; to stain, sully, disgrace.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گَنْدَه (ganda). First attested in c. 1679 as Middle Hindi گندہ (gndh /⁠ganda⁠/).

def: s.m. The cheek; side of the face; — a boil, a pimple; a goitre, or other excrescence of the neck; — a mark, spot; — a rhinoceros
subs: s.m. The cheek; the temple of an elephant
s.f. & m. Inflammation of the glands of the neck; goitre, bronchocele; — scrofula.

def: s.m. Sugar-cane, &c. (i.q. gannā, q.v.). s.m. Sugar-cane, Saccharum officinarum; — (dialec.) a reed; a cane; reed-grass (rustic forms are gaṇḍā; gāṅḍā)
Sant. n. m. S. गुडदण्ड Sugarcane ( īkh ).

ety: ganda, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Stink, stench; rottenness, putrefaction; — filth; ordure
more: n. f. 1. Stink; stench. 2. gandgī phailānā , v. a. To make a place filthy.
subs: To strew filth about; to make a place filthy, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian گندگی (gandagī).

def: s.m. = gindar, q.v. s.m. A kind of insect which is very destructive to growing 'gram' and arhar.

ety: S. गन्धकः, by change of gender; P. also gandak
def: s.f. Sulphur, brimstone; gunpowder
more: n. f. S. गन्धक Sulphur; brimstone. Biyāj baḍhāve dhan ghanā, or bheṛ baḍhāve chho, Jaise gaṅdrak āg māṅ gire to dūnī ho . Rus. With interest wealth grows, and fight with anger grows, As fire with sulphur fed with heat intenser glows. gandak kā tel yā tezāb , n. m. Oil of vitriol; sulphuric acid.
(see گندهک) s. f. Brimstone. گندک کا عطر gandak kā aﻌt̤r, Sulphurick acid.
subs: or gandak-kā tez-āb, Oil of vitriol; vitriolic acid; sulphuric acid

def: s.m. = gaṇḍakī, q.v. s.f. Name of a river in the northern part of India.
s.m. A rhinoceros; — an epithet of the people of Videha, living on the river Gaṇḍakī.
s.m. corr. of gaṇḍak, q.v. s.f. Sulphur, brimstone; gunpowder
subs: or gandak-kā tez-āb, Oil of vitriol; vitriolic acid; sulphuric acid

def: s.f. Name of a river in the northern part of India.
s.f. A kind of grass, Andropogon muricatum (used in thatching; and the root of which, known as ḵẖas-ḵẖas, is used for ṭat̤ṭīs).
s. m. (dakh.) Calamity, misfortune: see آفت āfat.

v. a. (dakh.) See گوندهنا gūndhnā, sign. 2.

def: n. f. Thatching grass, the root of which, or khas is used for ṭaṭīs; Andropogon muricatum. Gāṅḍar ke pūle .

def: s.m. The receptacle for the sugar-cane at the mill before it is cut up.

s.m. = gaṅḍarālā q.v. s.m. The receptacle for the sugar-cane at the mill before it is cut up.

more: s. m. A sort of axe, a poleaxe.

def: s.m. A rustic form of gaṅḍāsā, q.v.
E. n. m. 1. A chopper for cutting fodder. 2. A battle axe.

s.f. = ganḍerī, gaṛerī, qq.v.
more: s. f. A cut or portion of the sugar-cane. See گنڐيري ganḍerī.

def: s.f. A joint, or piece, or segment of sugar-cane; — (dialec.) a knot; joint.
more: s. f. A segment or portion of sugar-cane.

ety: S. गन्धं, or गन्धः by change of gender
def: s.f. Smell, odour, scent, perfume, fragrance; bad smell, stink, stench
subs: s.m. The Gardenia florida (ʻindigenous in China, and cultivated throughout India on account of its large white fragrant flowers'); — sandal-wood; — any sweet-smelling flower
s.m. Sandal-wood; — a species of jasmine (cf. gandh-rāj)
s.m. A fragrant wood (as sandal, aloe-wood, &c.)
s.m. Sulphur; — name of a mountain (prob. as being the source of sulphur); — a name of Rāvaṇ; — name of a certain monkey
s.m. 'The wafter of fragrance,' the wind; the zephyr; — adj. Fragrant.

def: n. m. 1. Smell ( bās ). 2. Sandalwood or other odorous matter ground and rubbed on the person or on idols. gandh na suhānā , v. a. To shun one's company.
adj. (f. -ī) = ganda, q.v.
subs: s.m. corr. of ganda-biroza or fīroza, q.v.s.v. ganda.

def: s.f. (in H. but m. in S.) Sulphur ( = gandak).
wety: From Sanskrit गन्धक (gandhaka, “sulfur”).

more: Sant. n. m. The flying bug.

def: s.m. The third of the seven primary notes of music; — name of a people and their country, commonly called Kandahār, and lying between the north of India and Persia; — name of one of the princes of that country.
more: n. m. One of the seven notes of the gamut.

def: s.m. Name of a rāg or musical mode.
more: n. m. S. गान्धार A rāg or musical mode.
s. m. The name of a Rāg or musical mode.

def: s.m. (f. -ī), An aërial minstrel, a kind of celestial musician; a class or (one of a class) of demi-gods who inhabit the heaven of Indra and constitute the orchestra at all the banquets of the principal deities; — the soul after death and previous to its being born again (according to Hindū notions); — a horse; — a kind of deer
more: n. m. A musician or singer in Indra's Court and in Hin. temples. gandharb bivāh . A marriage contracted without the usual ceremonies; a kind of Scotch marriage.

s. m. A celestial musician, a class of demi-gods who inhabit Indraʼs heaven, and form the orchestra at the banquets of the principal deities. گندهرب بياه gandharb-byāh, A kind of marriage. گندهرب گيت gandharb-gīt, A gandharb- song, a kind of song or singing.

or gandharb-biwāh, or gandharb-byāh, s.m. Marriage from mutual agreement (before witnesses or in writing, and without the usual ceremonies).
